questions.question: Explain windowed computations in Storm. Compare tumbling, sliding, and session windows with examples for IoT data.
keyPoints: Tumbling Windows: Fixed-size, non-overlapping (e.g., [0-10s], [10-20s]); used for simple periodic aggregations like hourly totals
Sliding Windows: Fixed-size, overlapping, slide at intervals (e.g., 60s window sliding every 10s); used for moving averages, trend detection
Session Windows: Dynamic size based on activity, close after inactivity timeout; used for user session analysis, event clustering
IoT Example: Smart meter with sliding 60s window computes moving average power consumption, tumbling 1-hour window for billing totals
Implementation: Maintain in-memory buffer, remove expired data based on timestamp, emit aggregation at window boundaries

questions.question: Describe how to build a complete Storm topology for real-time industrial equipment monitoring. Include Kafka integration, anomaly detection, windowed aggregation, and alert generation. Explain the role of each component and appropriate stream groupings.
keyPoints: Overall Architecture: IoT Sensors → Kafka → Storm Topology → Alerts/Storage/Dashboard
Kafka Integration: KafkaSpout reads from 'industrial-sensors' topic, provides reliable buffering, message persistence for replay, decouples data producers from Storm processing
Spout Component: IndustrialSensorSpout with parallelism=2, reads sensor data (machine_id, sensor_type, value, timestamp), emits with message_id for guaranteed processing
Validation Bolt: Checks value ranges (vibration 0-100, temperature -20-200), filters invalid data, uses shuffle grouping for load balancing, parallelism=4
Anomaly Detection Bolt: Threshold-based (critical>85, warning>70) + statistical analysis (z-score>3 sigma), maintains history per sensor, shuffle grouping, parallelism=8
Windowed Aggregation Bolt: 60s sliding window (10s slide), calculates avg/min/max/count per machine-sensor, uses fields grouping on (machine_id, sensor_type) for per-sensor state, parallelism=8
Alert Bolt: Generates alerts for critical anomalies, implements cooldown period (5 min) to prevent spam, emits to 'alerts' stream, parallelism=2
Storage Bolt: Batch writes to InfluxDB (100 records), stores aggregated metrics for dashboards, shuffle grouping, parallelism=4
Performance: Handles 10,000 machines × 3 sensors × 1 reading/sec = 30,000 events/sec with <100ms latency
Reliability: Tuple tracking, ack/fail mechanism, 30s timeout, guaranteed at-least-once processing
